steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content: Square $\sqrt{k-x}=62-x$ to clear the radical: $$k-x=(62-x)^2=3844-124x+x^2.$$ Note that any solution must also satisfy $62-x\ge 0$ so the right side is non-negative.

steps.title: Apply the unique-solution condition
steps.content: The smallest $k$ giving a valid solution is the tangency case $\Delta=0$: $$4k-247=0 \Rightarrow 4k=247.$$ The repeated root $x=\tfrac{123}{2}=61.5$ satisfies $x\le 62$, so it is valid. The answer is 247.
